name: Bifunctor

x.formalRole: A type constructor of two arguments that is functorial in both — you can map over either or both positions independently.
x.compositionRule: bimap : (a → c) → (b → d) → F a b → F c d; with first / second mapping one side.
laws: bimap (g∘h) (i∘j) = bimap g i ∘ bimap h j
x.encoding: Instances for pairs/tuples, Either/Result, and tagged unions. C++/Rust: map_err/map on Result; C#/Java via wrapper helpers.
x.useCases: Transforming both sides of an Either/Result (e.g. map the error AND the value), reshaping key-value pairs.
misuseRisks: Hand-rolling separate maps where bimap expresses intent

def: A functor in two type parameters: it provides a lawful way to map functions over either or both of its positions.
problem: Cleanly transform both components of a two-sided structure (like Result’s ok and error) without unwrapping.
mechanism: Supplies bimap (and the one-sided first/second). Laws mirror the functor laws on each position, guaranteeing identity and composition are preserved.
benefits: Single lawful operation over both sides
limits: Often under-used / unnamed in mainstream code
failures: Implementations that break the composition law
Confusing bimap with mapping just the value
myths: "Result.map covers everything" — mapping the error side is the bifunctor’s second half
